---

$650 billion flowing into AI infrastructure this year.

Bridgewater Associates just confirmed the scale: Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, and Microsoft will collectively invest approximately $650 billion in AI-related infrastructure in 2026. That is a 60% increase over 2025.

Bridgewater co-CIO Greg Jensen is calling it a "more dangerous phase" — not because AI is failing, but because the investment is accelerating beyond what most observers expected. Physical infrastructure. Data centers. Power systems. Cooling. Networking. All scaling at unprecedented rates.

For business leaders, this creates a dual reality. On one side, massively expanded compute capacity will drive inference costs down, making enterprise AI deployment more accessible than ever. On the other, the hyperscalers building these rails will aggressively monetize them — meaning the terms you negotiate today define your cost structure for years.

The strategic window is open. The question is whether your organization is positioned to move through it.

GYanTech AI works with executive teams to turn infrastructure shifts into deployment advantage. Link in bio.

## Fact-check notes
- Bridgewater Associates analysis reported by Reuters on Feb 23, 2026: Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, and Microsoft expected to collectively invest ~$650 billion in AI-related infrastructure in 2026 (Source: Reuters)
- This represents a ~60% increase from $410 billion in combined AI infrastructure spending in 2025 (Source: Reuters, Bridgewater Associates letter to clients)
- Bridgewater co-CIO Greg Jensen stated the AI boom has entered a 'more dangerous phase' marked by exponentially rising investments in physical infrastructure and growing reliance on outside capital (Source: Reuters)
